originalTitle: S&P 500, Nasdaq post best quarter since 2020 despite Iran war
summary: STORY: U.S. stocks ended the second quarter on a high note... with the Dow on Tuesday adding about a quarter of a percent to notch another record high close. The blue-chip index also registered its biggest quarterly jump in four years. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 rallied roughly eight-tenths of a percent, and the Nasdaq climbed more than one-and-a-half percent - with both indexes recording their biggest quarterly gains in six years. :: Archive Investor optimism about about economic and earnings growth has helped power stocks even amid the Middle East conflict. But Eric Diton, president and managing director of The Wealth Alliance, remains cautious about a pricey market that keeps climbing higher. "Now, what can stop it? No one rings a bell at the top. No one rings a bell at the bottom. But I will tell you that there are multiple just signs of froth. Signs of froth. // If you go back to Warren Buffett, no one wants to hear about Warren Buffett, but he's still the greatest investor in history in my book. And the Buffett Indicator just very simply, market value versus GDP. He says, when you're over 150%, that's a very overvalued market. Okay, well, what if you're at 218%? What's that?!" Investors have been particularly concerned about lofty valuations in the tech sector and continued massive spending on AI by those companies. Strategists at BofA said cyclical, value-oriented sectors such as energy and financials could be the better bet heading into the second half. As for Tuesday's notable stock moves, shares of Nike, down 1% at the close, tumbled at least another 8% in extended trading, after the world's largest sportswear company reported quarterly revenue that edged past estimates, but said its sales continued to fall in China, which has been a weak spot for Nike in recent quarters.

chineseTitle: 尽管伊朗战争，标普 500 指数和纳斯达克指数仍录得 2020 年以来最佳季度表现
chineseSummary: 故事：美国股市第二季度高收，道指周二上涨约 25%，创下收盘新高。该蓝筹股指数也录得四年来最大季度涨幅。与此同时，标准普尔 500 指数上涨了大约十分之八，纳斯达克指数上涨了超过百分之一半——这两个指数都创下了六年来最大的季度涨幅。即使在中东冲突期间，投资者对经济和盈利增长的乐观情绪也帮助股市上涨。但财富联盟总裁兼董事总经理埃里克·迪顿 (Eric Diton) 对不断攀升的昂贵市场仍持谨慎态度。 “现在，什么可以阻止它？没有人在顶部敲响警钟。没有人在底部敲响警钟。但我会告诉你，有多种只是泡沫的迹象。泡沫的迹象。//如果你回到沃伦·巴菲特，没有人想听到沃伦·巴菲特，但他仍然是我书中历史上最伟大的投资者。巴菲特指标非常简单，市场价值与 GDP 之比。他说，当你超过 150% 时，那就是一个非常大的投资者。好吧，如果你的价格是 218%呢？！”投资者特别担心科技行业的高估值以及这些公司在人工智能上持续大量支出。美国银行策略师表示，进入下半年，能源和金融等周期性、价值导向型行业可能是更好的选择。至于周二值得注意的股价走势，耐克股价收盘下跌 1%，在盘后交易中又下跌至少 8%，此前这家全球最大的运动服装公司公布的季度收入略高于预期，但表示其在中国的销售额继续下降，而中国是耐克最近一个季度的弱点。

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
